steps.title: Find ST using the area formula
steps.content:
Since angle $T$ is a right angle, the triangle has two perpendicular legs: $\overline{RT}$ and $\overline{ST}$. The area of a right triangle equals $\frac{1}{2}$ times the product of its legs.
$$468 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 72 \cdot ST$$
Solving for $ST$:
$$936 = 72 \cdot ST$$
$$ST = 13$$

steps.title: Recognize similar triangles from the parallel condition
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, with $L$ on $\overline{RS}$ and $K$ on $\overline{ST}$, triangle $SKL$ is similar to triangle $STR$.

Why? Two reasons:
  • Both triangles share angle $S$ at vertex $S$
  • Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, corresponding angles are equal: $\angle LKS = \angle RTS = 90°$
By AA similarity, $\triangle SKL \sim \triangle STR$.

steps.title: Apply the similarity ratio
steps.content:
For similar triangles, corresponding sides are proportional. The ratio of similarity comes from any pair of corresponding sides:
$$\frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{24}{72} = \frac{1}{3}$$
This same ratio applies to all pairs of corresponding sides. In particular:
$$\frac{SK}{ST} = \frac{1}{3}$$
So:
$$SK = \frac{1}{3} \cdot 13 = \frac{13}{3}$$

steps.title: Calculate KT
steps.content:
Since $K$ lies on segment $\overline{ST}$ between $S$ and $T$, the length $KT$ is the remainder of $ST$ after subtracting $SK$:
$$KT = ST - SK = 13 - \frac{13}{3}$$
Converting to a single fraction:
$$KT = \frac{39}{3} - \frac{13}{3} = \frac{26}{3}$$
Acceptable forms: 26/3, 8.666, or 8.667.
